label: Fukushima Daiichi NPP
sub: Six-unit plant; Units 1–4 severely damaged. Site of ongoing decommissioning and ALPS water storage.

label: Fukushima Daini NPP
sub: Four-unit plant safely shut down during the disaster; NRA ordered permanent closure in 2019. TEPCO decommissioning in progress.

label: Okuma Town (Hard-to-Return Zone)
sub: Hosts the Daiichi plant; most of town closed since 2011. Small special reconstruction zone opened 2023. Former residents largely have not returned.

label: Futaba Town (Hard-to-Return Zone)
sub: Hosts part of Daiichi plant; remains Japan's most heavily restricted municipality. Interim storage facility for contaminated soil located here.

label: Namie Town (Partial Return)
sub: Most evacuation orders lifted March 2017; hard-to-return zones still closed. Population return rate ~14% as of 2023.

label: Tomioka Town (Partial Return)
sub: Partial evacuation order lifted April 2017 for decontaminated areas; northern districts remain restricted. Return rate ~22% as of 2023.

label: Naraha Town (Returned Sept 2015)
sub: First entire town to have evacuation order lifted (Sept 5, 2015). Return rate reached ~65% by 2022. 20 km south of Daiichi.

label: Iitate Village (Planned Evacuation Zone)
sub: 40 km NW of Daiichi; designated Planned Evacuation Zone April 2011 due to heavy Cs-137 fallout from NW wind plume. Evacuation orders lifted 2017 for most areas.

label: Minamisoma City
sub: Straddles former exclusion zone boundary; northern Odaka district evacuated. Major monitoring hub; partial liftings through 2017. Active radiation monitoring network.

label: J-Village (Emergency Staging Area)
sub: Japan's national football training centre converted to emergency response hub March 2011. Workers decontaminated and bused to plant from here. Reopened as sports facility 2019.

label: Fukushima City (Prefecture Capital)
sub: 60 km W of Daiichi; prefecture capital. Received measurable Cs-137 deposition; widespread decontamination. City now well below intervention levels. Ongoing monitoring.

label: Katsurao Village
sub: Located in heavy contamination zone NW of plant. Evacuation order lifted 2016; ongoing decontamination of forests surrounding village.

label: Sendai (Regional Hub)
sub: Miyagi Prefecture capital; 90 km north of Daiichi. Major regional coordination hub for disaster response. Sustained infrastructure damage from 2011 tsunami. Ongoing monitoring station.

label: Iwaki City
sub: Fukushima's largest city; 40 km south of Daiichi. Served as major evacuation destination. Monitoring station; radiation levels remain low.

label: Soma City
sub: Coastal city 40 km north of Daiichi. Sustained severe tsunami damage but outside primary exclusion zone. Ongoing environmental monitoring; fisheries recovery focal point.

label: Nihonmatsu City (Decontaminated)
sub: 55 km W of Daiichi; received cesium fallout from NW plume. Intensive decontamination conducted 2012–2014. Now below 0.23 μSv/h intervention standard.

label: ALPS Water Undersea Discharge Point
sub: Submarine pipeline extends 1 km offshore from Daiichi. ALPS-treated water discharged here since Aug 24, 2023. IAEA and TEPCO monitor daily tritium levels in surrounding ocean.

label: Fukushima Interim Storage Facility (FISTF)
sub: 16 km² facility in former exclusion zones of Futaba and Okuma. Stores ~14 million 1-tonne bags of contaminated soil from decontamination operations. Government required to remove waste by 2045.

label: 20 km Exclusion Zone (Former Boundary)
sub: Circular 20 km exclusion zone established March 12, 2011. Covered ~1,150 km² and included about 78,000 residents. Zone boundaries later replaced by municipality-specific designations.

label: Onagawa NPP (Safely Shut Down)
sub: Tohoku Electric Power Co. plant 130 km north of Daiichi. Although closer to the earthquake epicenter, withstood the tsunami due to higher seawall; safely shut down. Became temporary refuge for tsunami survivors.
